description_long: This object is a sculpted guard stone in the form of a stylized animal, likely a dog or jackal, with upright pointed ears and a seated posture. The surface is dark, matte, and appears to be made of weathered stone, showing subtle chisel marks and a slightly rough texture. The body is blocky and geometric, with simplified anatomical features and a solid, monolithic build. The head is elongated with a pronounced snout and deep-set eyes, while the legs are thick and merge into a broad base for stability. The overall form is imposing and functional, designed to serve as a protective barrier at the edge of a pathway or entrance. Its visual style suggests historical or traditional influences, possibly referencing ancient architectural motifs.
description: A dark, stone guard stone sculpted as a stylized seated animal with pointed ears and a robust, geometric form. The surface is rough and weathered, evoking a sense of durability and historical character.
description_view_0: Front view shows the symmetrical, upright animal figure with pointed ears, broad chest, and front legs together.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the elongated snout, deep-set eye, and the animal's seated posture with a sloped back.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the wide, flat back and the solid, monolithic structure of the object.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the same stylized head and body contours.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the corners of urban streets, at the entrance to driveways, or flanking the entryways of historical buildings to prevent vehicle encroachment and protect structures.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright on a flat surface, sometimes partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Surface may show chipping and smoothing at the base and corners from vehicle contact and foot traffic, with possible discoloration or erosion from prolonged exposure to weather elements. The top and edges may be more polished from incidental contact.
